description:
Patient John Zenon, a chemical maintenance technician, sustained a 2% body surface area splash of Hydrofluoric Acid (HF) to his right forearm. HF is highly toxic and causes severe systemic hypocalcemia. As the Occupational Health provider, complete the HF exposure protocol in GNU Health. Requirements include documenting the toxicological diagnosis (ICD-10 T54.2 or T22), recording a clinical evaluation of acute pain and tachycardia (Heart Rate >= 100 bpm), prescribing 'Calcium Gluconate 2.5% Gel', ordering at least two laboratory tests to monitor systemic toxicity (including a Calcium level or CMP), and scheduling a wound reassessment appointment for one day from today.
